page.title: Astondoa 40 vs Pershing 46: Cost Comparison for Marbella Charters

data.meta_description: Compare Astondoa 40 vs Pershing 46 charter costs in Marbella. See €749/2h vs €2,299/8h pricing, specs, and which boat suits your group. Book now.
data.summary: A cost comparison of the Astondoa 40 and Pershing 46 for Marbella boat charters, covering pricing, capacity, and inclusions to help you choose.
key_facts: Astondoa 40: €749 for 2 hours, €2,299 for 8 hours (all inclusive).
Pershing 46: pricing on request via WhatsApp (no fixed hourly rates).
Astondoa 40 seats up to 9 passengers; Pershing 46 seats up to 10.
Both depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella.
Every charter includes licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and VAT.
Astondoa 40 is a Tier A boat; Pershing 46 is a premium sport yacht.
Pershing 46 offers higher speed (40+ knots) and luxury finish.
Astondoa 40 is more budget-friendly for shorter trips.

faq.q: What is the cost of chartering the Astondoa 40 in Marbella?
faq.a: The Astondoa 40 costs €749 for 2 hours, €1,299 for 4 hours, €1,799 for 6 hours, and €2,299 for 8 hours. All prices include a skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and 21% Spanish VAT.

faq.q: How much does it cost to charter the Pershing 46 in Marbella?
faq.a: Pricing for the Pershing 46 is available on request via WhatsApp. It is not listed with fixed hourly rates, as it is a premium sport yacht. Contact us for a custom quote based on your trip duration and requirements.

faq.q: Which boat is bigger: Astondoa 40 or Pershing 46?
faq.a: The Astondoa 40 is 12.5 metres long, while the Pershing 46 is 14 metres long. The Pershing 46 is slightly larger and can accommodate up to 10 passengers, compared to the Astondoa 40’s 9 passengers.

faq.q: Can I charter the Pershing 46 for a shorter trip like 2 hours?
faq.a: Yes, shorter trips are possible, but the Pershing 46 is typically booked for longer charters (4 hours or more) due to its higher performance and luxury. Contact us via WhatsApp to discuss options.

faq.q: What is included in the charter price for the Astondoa 40?
faq.a: The price includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden extras.

faq.q: Is the Pershing 46 faster than the Astondoa 40?
faq.a: Yes, the Pershing 46 is a high-performance sport yacht capable of speeds over 40 knots, whereas the Astondoa 40 cruises at around 20-25 knots. The Pershing 46 is better for thrill-seekers.

faq.q: Which boat is better for a large group in Marbella?
faq.a: Both are suitable. The Astondoa 40 seats 9 passengers, while the Pershing 46 seats 10. If you have a group of 10, the Pershing 46 is the better choice. For smaller groups, the Astondoa 40 offers excellent value.

faq.q: How do I book the Astondoa 40 or Pershing 46?
faq.a: You can book the Astondoa 40 directly through our website with fixed pricing. For the Pershing 46, contact us via WhatsApp for a custom quote and availability. Both depart from Puerto Banús.

data.body_html:
Choosing between the Astondoa 40 and the Pershing 46 for your Marbella charter often comes down to budget and experience. The Astondoa 40 offers transparent, all-inclusive pricing starting at €749 for 2 hours, while the Pershing 46 is a premium sport yacht with bespoke rates. This comparison breaks down costs, specs, and value so you can decide which boat suits your group and wallet.

Astondoa 40 vs Pershing 46: Key Specs

Both boats depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella, but they cater to different needs. The Astondoa 40 (12.5m) is a classic flybridge cruiser, ideal for relaxed coastal cruising. The Pershing 46 (14m) is a high-performance sport yacht, built for speed and luxury. Here’s a quick comparison:

  • Length: Astondoa 40 (12.5m) vs Pershing 46 (14m)
  • Passenger capacity: Astondoa 40 (9 pax) vs Pershing 46 (10 pax)
  • Speed: Astondoa 40 (cruise 20-25 knots) vs Pershing 46 (40+ knots)
  • Departure point: Both from Puerto Banús
  • Inclusions: Both include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and VAT

Cost Breakdown: Astondoa 40

The Astondoa 40 is a Tier A boat with fixed, all-inclusive pricing. No surprises. You pay one price that covers everything:

DurationPrice (€)
2 hours€749
4 hours€1,299
6 hours€1,799
8 hours€2,299

These rates include a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). Perfect for budget-conscious groups who want total transparency.

Cost Breakdown: Pershing 46

The Pershing 46 is a premium sport yacht, and its pricing is on request via WhatsApp. We don’t list fixed hourly rates because each charter is customised—longer trips, special requests, or seasonal demand may affect the quote. Typically, expect a higher per-hour cost than the Astondoa 40, reflecting its performance and luxury. Contact us for a personalised quote.

What’s Included in Both Charters?

Every charter from our fleet includes the same core package:

  • Licensed skipper
  • Fuel for the trip
  • Water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava
  • Light snacks
  • Insurance and safety equipment
  • Spanish IVA (21%)

No hidden extras. Whether you choose the Astondoa 40 or Pershing 46, you get a worry-free experience departing from Puerto Banús.

Which Boat Offers Better Value?

Value depends on your priorities. The Astondoa 40 is unbeatable for fixed pricing and short trips. At €749 for 2 hours, it’s ideal for a quick sunset cruise along the Marbella coastline. The Pershing 46, while more expensive, delivers a thrill with speeds over 40 knots and a sleek Italian design. If you want to impress guests or enjoy a high-speed ride to Cabopino or Estepona, the Pershing 46 is worth the premium.

For groups of 9 or fewer, the Astondoa 40 is cost-effective. For 10 passengers or those seeking luxury, the Pershing 46 is the better choice. Both boats depart from Puerto Banús, so location is identical.

Where Do They Cruise?

Both charters start at Puerto Banús, Marbella’s iconic marina. Popular routes include cruising past the Golden Mile, anchoring at Cala del Faro, or heading to Cabopino for lunch. The Pershing 46 can cover more ground quickly, reaching Sotogrande or Estepona in less time. The Astondoa 40 is perfect for a leisurely pace, soaking up views of La Concha mountain and the Mediterranean.

How to Book

Booking the Astondoa 40 is straightforward with fixed prices on our site. For the Pershing 46, simply contact us via WhatsApp for a custom quote. Both boats are available for half-day or full-day charters. Check availability and secure your date—Marbella’s peak season books fast.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the cost of chartering the Astondoa 40 in Marbella?

The Astondoa 40 costs €749 for 2 hours, €1,299 for 4 hours, €1,799 for 6 hours, and €2,299 for 8 hours. All prices include a skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and 21% Spanish VAT.

How much does it cost to charter the Pershing 46 in Marbella?

Pricing for the Pershing 46 is available on request via WhatsApp. It is not listed with fixed hourly rates, as it is a premium sport yacht. Contact us for a custom quote based on your trip duration and requirements.

Which boat is bigger: Astondoa 40 or Pershing 46?

The Astondoa 40 is 12.5 metres long, while the Pershing 46 is 14 metres long. The Pershing 46 is slightly larger and can accommodate up to 10 passengers, compared to the Astondoa 40’s 9 passengers.

Can I charter the Pershing 46 for a shorter trip like 2 hours?

Yes, shorter trips are possible, but the Pershing 46 is typically booked for longer charters (4 hours or more) due to its higher performance and luxury. Contact us via WhatsApp to discuss options.

What is included in the charter price for the Astondoa 40?

The price includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden extras.

Is the Pershing 46 faster than the Astondoa 40?

Yes, the Pershing 46 is a high-performance sport yacht capable of speeds over 40 knots, whereas the Astondoa 40 cruises at around 20-25 knots. The Pershing 46 is better for thrill-seekers.

Which boat is better for a large group in Marbella?

Both are suitable. The Astondoa 40 seats 9 passengers, while the Pershing 46 seats 10. If you have a group of 10, the Pershing 46 is the better choice. For smaller groups, the Astondoa 40 offers excellent value.

How do I book the Astondoa 40 or Pershing 46?

You can book the Astondoa 40 directly through our website with fixed pricing. For the Pershing 46, contact us via WhatsApp for a custom quote and availability. Both depart from Puerto Banús.
